text:
In the serene village of Arborville, everyone knew each other's names. At the heart of this close-knit community was a beloved fox named Felix. Felix didn't just walk; he roamed the woods in shimmering cloak, always cheerful, wearing a little golden collar.

One crisp autumn morning, Felix found himself wandering outside Mrs. Thompson's apple orchard. He had always loved the way those fruits fell into his mouth. As he watched an apple fall, he decided it needed a little adventure of its own. Felix leaped onto the branch.

Just then, a branch snapped, startling him. He glanced down to see none other than young Lily peering down at him. She giggled, a sound as sweet as autumn leaves.

"Lift! Lift!" she insisted. Felix blinked. How could a fox lift? But he felt a playful tug inside him, so he lifted his shoulder.

"You've got strength, Mr. Felix!" Lily exclaimed, thrilled.
